title: 30-Day Hospital Readmission Insights and Cost Trends
description:
Project Summary: Predicting Hospital Readmissions Using AI and Patient History
Can we predict which patients are likely to bounce back into the hospital — before they ever leave?
This end-to-end data science and business intelligence project takes on one of healthcare’s costliest and most persistent problems: unplanned 30-day hospital readmissions.

By combining machine learning, Python, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI, this project transforms raw hospital admission data into powerful clinical insights. We train and compare Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers on patient demographics and clinical profiles to forecast high-risk discharges. The results? A dynamic, executive-level dashboard that reveals not only who is most at risk — but why.

From readmission rates and patient volume to cost drivers and risk segments, stakeholders can explore patterns, act on predictive indicators, and intervene early to save lives and resources. Whether you're a data scientist, healthcare analyst, or policymaker, this project offers a replicable blueprint for smarter, value-based care powered by data.

Step Name:Step 1 – Import Data
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:

1. Open Power BI Desktop.

2. Click on the red button  to get the data.

3. Click on the Home tab and select Get Data.

4. Choose the Excel workbook from the list of data sources.

5. Navigate to the file named PatientAdmission_dts.xlsx and click Open.

6. In the Navigator window, check the relevant sheet or table (e.g., PatientAdmission_dts).

7. Click Load to import the data into Power BI.

Insight: 
Import Data Into PowerBi BI

Step Name:Step 2 – Remove Duplicate Records
Detailed Instructions:
 Instructions:

In Power BI Desktop, click Transform Data to open Power Query Editor.
In the data table, locate and select the Encounter ID column.
Right-click the Encounter ID column header.
Choose Remove Duplicates from the dropdown menu to eliminate any duplicate rows.

Insight: 

Data Transformation in Power BI

Step Name:Filter Out Placeholder Values
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
In Power BI Desktop, click Transform Data to open Power Query Editor.
Select the BillingAmount column and click the filter dropdown.
Uncheck null to exclude missing billing amounts.
Repeat this for the Race column by selecting its filter dropdown.
Uncheck ? to exclude unknown race entries.
Click Close & Apply to apply the filters and load the cleaned data.

Insight: 

Data Transformation

Step Name:Create Date Table
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
In Power BI Desktop, go to the Model or Data view.
Click the Modeling tab on the top ribbon.
Select New Table from the ribbon.
In the formula bar, paste the following DAX expression:
DateTable = 

VAR MinDate =

    MIN ( 'PatientAdmission_dts'[AdmissionDate] ) 

VAR MaxDate =

    MAX ( 'PatientAdmission_dts'[DischargeDate]) 

RETURN

ADDCOLUMNS (

    CALENDAR ( MinDate, MaxDate ), 

    "Year",          YEAR ( [Date] ),

    "Quarter",       "Q" & FORMAT ( [Date], "Q" ),

    "MonthNumber",   MONTH ( [Date] ),

    "MonthName",     FORMAT ( [Date], "MMMM" ),

    "MonthYear",     FORMAT ( [Date], "MMM yyyy" ),

    "WeekdayName",   FORMAT ( [Date], "dddd" ),

    "IsMonthEnd",    IF ( EOMONTH ( [Date], 0 ) = [Date], 1, 0 ),

    "IsQuarterEnd",  IF ( EOMONTH ( [Date], 0 ) = [Date]

                         && MONTH ( [Date] ) IN {3,6,9,12}, 1, 0 )

)

5.      Press Enter to create the custom Date Table in your model.

Insight: 
Creating Data Table In Power BI

Step Name:Build Relationships
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
In Power BI Desktop, click the Model icon on the left panel to open the relationship view.
Locate the Date column in the DateTable and drag it onto the AdmissionDate column in the PatientAdmission_dts table.
In the relationship dialog, ensure the relationship is set to single-directional and marked as active.
Confirm the relationship is valid, then click OK.
Click Save to preserve the relationship in your model.

Insight: 

Building Relationships Between Table

Step Name:Create KPI Table and Measures
Detailed Instructions:
 

Instructions:
In Power BI Desktop, click the Modeling tab from the top ribbon.
Click New Table to create a container table for KPIs.
Enter the following DAX formula:
KPI_Metrics = { "Metric" }

4.        Press Enter to add the KPI table.
5.      With KPI_Metrics selected in the Fields pane, click New Measure.
6.      Enter the following DAX to calculate 30-Day Readmission Rate
30-Day Readmission Rate % = 
DIVIDE (
    CALCULATE (
        COUNTROWS ( 'PatientAdmission_dts' ),
        'PatientAdmission_dts'[Readmitted] = "<30"
    ),
    COUNTROWS ( 'PatientAdmission_dts' )
)
7.      Press Enter to save the measure.
8.       Repeat steps 5–7 for all other required KPIs, using the same method.

Insight: 

Create KPI Table and Measures

Step Name:Format Report Page and Canvas
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
Click the Format page icon (paint roller) in the Visualizations pane.
Under Canvas settings:
Type: Custom, Height: 800 px, Width: 1366 px, Alignment: Top
Under Canvas background:
Color: #E1FAF2, Image: Upload (clipart280787.png), Fit: Normal, Transparency: 96%

Step Name:Add and Position Logo
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
Go to Home > Insert > Image and upload your logo (e.g., hospital_logo.png).
With the image selected, open the Format image pane.
Under Size: Height = 96, Width = 147, Lock aspect ratio = On.
Under Position: Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 0.

Insight: IMPORT IMAGES into POWER BI // DIFFERENT WAYS to Import and Use Images in Power BI Reports

Step Name:Add Title Text Box
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
Click Home > Text box, then enter:
30-Day Hospital Readmission Insights and Cost Trends
Highlight the text and apply:
 Font: Calibri, Size: 32 pt, Bold, Color: #008080, Alignment: Center
With the text box selected, open Format > General:
Size: Height = 81, Width = 1056
Position: Horizontal = 196, Vertical = 0
Under Background: Toggle On, Color = #E1FAF2, Transparency = 96%

Insight: Master Power BI: Learn How to Add Text Boxes for Professional Data Visualizations

Step Name:Create and Format the Year Slicer
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
Click the Slicer icon from the Visualizations pane and add it to the canvas.
Drag Year from the Date table into the slicer field well.
Select the slicer and open Format visual (paint roller).
Under Visual:
Slicer settings: Style = Between
Slicer header: Off
Values: Font = Calibri, Size = 10 pt, Color = #666666, Background = #F8FFFD, Slider = #CCCCCC
Under General > Properties:
Size: Height = 99, Width = 201
Position: Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 81
Under Title:
Toggle On, Text = Year, Font = DIN 12 pt, Bold
Text color = #FFFFFF, Background = #227049, Align = Center, Wrap = On
Under Effects:
Background: Color = #F8FFFD, Transparency = 90%
Border: Color = #CCCCCC, Width = 2 px

Insight: Power BI Slicers - A Step-by-Step Tutorial for All Types to Improve Data Analysis

Step Name:Create and Format the Total Patient KPI Card
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
Click the Card visual in the Visualizations pane.
Drag TotalPatients from KPI_Metrics into the card.
Select the card and open Format visual (paint roller).
Under Visual > Callout value:
Font = DIN, Size = 35 pt, Color = #557E66, Text wrap = On, Source spacing = On
Under General > Properties:
Size: Height = 113, Width = 201
Position: Horizontal = 0, Vertical = 207
Under Title:
On — Text = Total Patient, Heading 3
Font = DIN, Size = 12 pt, Text color = #FFFFFF, Background = #227049
Alignment = Center, Wrap = On
Under Effects:
Background: #DDF4F1, Transparency = 90%
Border: Color = #CCCCCC, Width = 2 px
Under Data format:
Field: TotalPatients, Format = Whole number
Thousands separator = On, Decimal places = 0

Insight: 

How to Create KPI Card

Project Summary: Predicting Hospital Readmissions Using AI and Patient History Can we predict which patients are likely to bounce back into the hospital — before they ever leave? This end-to-end data science and business intelligence project takes on one of healthcare’s costliest and most persistent problems: unplanned 30-day hospital readmissions. Step Name:Create the Billing Group Classification Column
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
In Power BI, go to Data view and select the PatientAdmission_dts table.
Click New column on the top ribbon.
Enter the following DAX formula:
BillingGroup = 
SWITCH(
    TRUE(),
    PatientAdmission_dts[BillingGroup] = "<1K", "Very Low",
    PatientAdmission_dts[BillingGroup] = "1K-10K", "Low",
    PatientAdmission_dts[BillingGroup] = "10K-30K", "Medium",
    PatientAdmission_dts[BillingGroup] IN { "30K-50K", ">50K" }, "High",
    BLANK()
)
    4. Press Enter to add the column.
    5. The new BillingGroup will now label each row by billing range category.

Insight: Introduction to Power BI: Add a new column

Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:
Insert a Combo Chart (Line and Stacked Column).
Build the chart with:
X-axis: MonthName
Column y-axis: TotalPatients

Column legend: Billing_Group
Tooltips: YoY_HighRisk %, YoY_ReadmitRate
Format axes:
X-axis: Font = Calibri 9 pt, Color = #444444, Max Height = 25%, Concatenate Labels = Off
Y-axis: Font = Calibri 9 pt, Units = Auto, Title = Off
Secondary Y-axis: Font = Calibri 9 pt, Align Zeros = On, Units = Auto, Title = Off
Legend: Position = Top left, Style = Marker (circle), Match Line Color = On, Font = Calibri 9 pt
Column colors by Billing_Group:
High = #386641, Medium = #6A994E, Low = #A7C957, Very Low = #F2E8CF
Line formatting:
30-Day Readmission %: Line = #E66C37, Width = 3 px, Markers = Dot, Size = 5 px
HighRisk %: Line = #E8D166, Width = 3 px, Markers = Dot, Size = 5 px
General > Properties:
Size = Height: 322, Width: 418
Position = Horizontal: 201, Vertical: 81
Title:
Text = 30-Day Readmission, Font = DIN 12 pt, Heading 3
Color = White, Background = #386641, Align = Center, Wrap = On

Step Name:Create SubKPI Table and Waterfall Measure
Detailed Instructions:

Instructions:

1. Go to Modeling > New Table and enter:
SubKPIs = 
DATATABLE(
    "KPI", STRING,
    {
        {"YoY_TotalPatients"},
        {"YoY_ReadmitRate %"},
        {"YoY_AvgLOS"},
        {"YoY_AvgCostReadmit"},
        {"YoY_HighRisk %"}
    }
)

2. Go to Modeling > New Measure and enter:
Waterfall_SubKPI_Values = 
SWITCH (
    SELECTEDVALUE ( SubKPIs[KPI] ),
    "YoY_TotalPatients", [YoY_TotalPatients],
    "YoY_ReadmitRate %", [YoY_ReadmitRate],
    "YoY_AvgLOS", [YoY_AvgLOS],
    "YoY_AvgCostReadmit", [YoY_AvgCostReadmit],
    "YoY_HighRisk %", [YoY_HighRisk %]
)

Insight: Guide to WATERFALL CHARTS in Power BI // Dynamic Categories and Measures with Field Parameters

portfolioContent.title: 30-Day Hospital Readmission Insights and Cost Trends
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed predictive models and interactive dashboards to reduce costly 30-day hospital readmissions using AI and clinical data insights.
portfolioContent.summary: This project leverages machine learning techniques, including Logistic Regression and Random Forest, to predict patients at high risk of 30-day hospital readmission. It integrates Python, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI to transform raw hospital data into actionable clinical insights via an executive dashboard. Stakeholders can explore readmission patterns, cost drivers, and risk segments to enable early intervention and improve patient outcomes.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Unplanned 30-day hospital readmissions represent a significant financial and clinical burden for healthcare providers. Identifying patients at risk before discharge remains a challenge, limiting opportunities for timely intervention. This project addresses the need for predictive tools to reduce readmission rates and associated costs.
objectives: Predict high-risk patient readmissions within 30 days using clinical and demographic data
Develop an interactive dashboard for stakeholders to explore risk factors and cost trends
Enable data-driven early interventions to reduce readmission rates and healthcare costs
workflow: Import and clean hospital admission data
Remove duplicates and filter placeholder values
Build data relationships and create date and KPI tables
Train and compare Logistic Regression and Random Forest models
Deploy interactive Power BI dashboard for executive insights
keyInsights: Certain patient demographics and clinical profiles strongly predict readmission risk
Readmission rates correlate with specific cost drivers and patient volume trends
Risk segmentation enables targeted interventions for high-risk groups
Dynamic dashboards improve stakeholder understanding of readmission patterns
businessImpact: Reduced unplanned 30-day readmissions through early risk identification
Lowered healthcare costs by targeting high-risk patients for intervention
Enhanced decision-making with actionable, data-driven clinical insights
